response.justification: The candidate is a versatile senior engineer with ~15 years of broad full-stack, embedded, and high-throughput backend experience, plus a genuine testing/craftsmanship orientation. However, the role is a Principal (IC6) position centered on experimentation platforms, telemetry foundations, feature flighting, and statistical analysis — domains absent from the resume. There is no evidence of experimentation systems work, growth engineering, metrics platform ownership, or leading multi-workstream initiatives across teams at enterprise scale, and most recent roles are senior IC scope on product teams rather than platform architecture leadership.
strengths: 15+ years of engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded, exceeding the 8-year bar
Low-latency, high-scale backend experience (micro-services handling millions of HTTP requests/second at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic)
Strong quality culture: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, open-source test tooling, and teaching/mentoring via workshops
concerns: No experimentation, A/B testing, feature-flagging, or growth-measurement platform experience — the core of the role
No demonstrated Principal-level scope: no multi-team initiative leadership or org-wide system ownership
Limited evidence of data/statistical analysis, telemetry infrastructure, or metrics-driven decision systems

key_factors.factor: Strong general engineering fundamentals at scale
